Role

Product Designer

Timeline

Aug 2023 – Jan 2024 · 4 months

Team

1 PM · 1 Eng Manager · Engineers

Tools

Figma · FigJam

Mode + Thoughtspot

Enterprise Analytics Data Tools UX Research

Mode Report Filters

Allowing users to easily navigate, refine, and coordinate data insights

What is Mode?

A tool for data analysts to use SQL, Python, and R, build custom visualizations, and prototype insights quickly. I was the sole product designer on the Dazery team, working alongside a PM and eng manager.

Problem

Users are overwhelmed sifting through data and unsure how to find more value in their findings.

Solution

New filtering capabilities — more ways to transform and understand what's going on in their data.

OUR KEY USER ↘

How can we consolidate the data and help present the best filtering options based on the type of data?

Phase 01 — Quick Fixes

Consolidating the filter experience

Phase 02 — New Features

How can we consolidate data and present the
best filtering options based on type?

Set Date Filters

Used for point in time analysis

Ex. April 1 – April 7 2024, stays constant once applied

Relative Date Filters

Auto updates with time

Ex. Past 7 days, Last quarter, Rolling window

Cascading filters

Dynamically update based on selections in other filters

Ex. Minimizes scroll through unneeded info

Display relevant values

Starting out, we engaged in extensive white boarding and brainstorming sessions to understand how the filters interact behind the scenes and determine what should or shouldn't be displayed to users.

And then we iterated, iterated, iterated

It came down to two main approaches:

Given the uncertainty about which direction was best, we decided to conduct user research for additional feedback.

Button Approach

Icon Approach

Sharing with users

We tested both options with 6 existing users from varying backgrounds.

4 people preferred the button over the icon

3 people discovered the icon without prompting

2 people thought icon was indicator instead of click point

lowhigh

The compactness of filters averaged at 3.6/5

"The double icon feels confusing. I'm not sure which one to press — I'm sure I could click and find out but on first glance I'm confused"

"The word customize feels very general... You aren't really customizing if you are just setting up the details of the filter"

Final Solutions

Updated UI

Redesigned filters now match the product's visual and interaction patterns for seamless integration.

Display relevant values

Allowing users to dynamically adjust filters based on data point selections giving business users the capabilities of the data analysts.

Relative date filters

Allowing users the ability to dynamically adjust data presentation based on current time frames.

Takeaways

Engaging users early and often was the biggest unlock. The iterative approach — continuous feedback and refinement — proved essential for building intuitive filter systems for complex data tools.

Balancing simplicity with functionality was a recurring theme throughout. Powerful filtering options shouldn't overwhelm users — getting that right took close collaboration across design, PM, and engineering.
